ranked_tasks.reasoning: This task carries a critical risk level (risk_001), has rolled over twice indicating persistent blockage, and is blocking 3 downstream dependencies on the NGIS Phase 3 project. The deadline of 2026-03-10 is imminent, the Director has already been briefed per governance committee notes, and the vendor delay directly threatens the overall migration timeline.

This task addresses a critical ITSG-33 IA-5 compliance finding (risk_002) flagged by TBS, with a deadline of 2026-03-12 and zero rollover, meaning it is newly surfaced but externally mandated with no flexibility. It blocks 2 dependencies and non-compliance with a TBS audit submission carries significant regulatory and reputational risk for the department.

This task has the earliest deadline in the entire list (2026-03-07) and is currently not started, making it immediately overdue or at extreme risk of being missed. Although it blocks no dependencies directly, it is a prerequisite analytical task that informs the resolution of task_001 and the Site A cutover planning; without this impact assessment, downstream decisions cannot be made with confidence.

A $340,000 (14%) budget overrun (risk_003) requires Director approval and has already rolled over once, indicating prior delay in resolution. The deadline is 2026-03-10 and the task is in progress, but failure to secure approval will block at least 1 dependent task and may further compound the financial risk on proj_002.

The potential departure of the sole AD migration expert (risk_004) represents a critical single point of failure for proj_001, and this task blocks the highest number of dependencies (4) of any task in the list. Although the deadline is 2026-03-17 and rollover is zero, the knowledge transfer window is narrow and the risk of losing institutional knowledge is irreversible once the expert departs.

This task has an imminent deadline of 2026-03-10 and is currently in progress, which reduces urgency somewhat, but it blocks 1 downstream dependency and is part of the NGIS Phase 3 project already under stress. Medium risk level and low stakeholder priority (2) prevent it from ranking higher, but the same-day deadline with task_001 and task_003 requires it to be completed in parallel today.

Despite a low risk classification, this task has rolled over 3 times — the highest rollover count in the list — which signals a persistent pattern of deferral that warrants attention. It has no hard deadline and blocks no dependencies, but its direct relevance to resolving the vendor delay (risk_001) and informing task_001 gives it moderate strategic value.

The Treasury Board submission for GC-Cloud Wave 4 has a deadline of 2026-03-15 and is not yet started, but carries medium risk with no dependencies blocked and no rollover history. TB submissions have fixed external deadlines and preparation time should be initiated promptly to avoid a last-minute compliance risk, but it does not carry the same urgency as the critical and high-priority items above.

This task has a distant deadline of 2026-04-01, low risk, no rollover, no blocked dependencies, and the lowest stakeholder priority (1). While end-user communication is important for migration success, there is sufficient lead time to address this after higher-priority items are resolved, and it does not carry any compliance or financial risk.

This is a purely administrative formatting task with no risk level assigned, no dependencies blocked, zero rollover, a distant deadline of 2026-04-15, and a stakeholder priority of zero. It should only be addressed once all substantive project work is complete and carries no operational, compliance, or financial consequence if deferred.
